sections.paragraph:
(“watch-tower,” “lofty place”), [Mizpah], pr.n.-

(1) of a town of Gilead, Jud. 10:17 11:11 , 34 Jdg 11:34 Hos 5:1 more fully, Jdg 11:29, מִצְפֵּה־גִלְעָד. As to the origin of this place, see Gen 31:49.-(2) of a town of the Benjamites, where the people were accustomed to assemble, Jud. 21:1 1Sa 7:5. It was afterwards fortified by Asa, to guard the frontiers against the kingdom of Israel ( 1Ki 15:22 2Ch 16:6); and at length it was made the seat of the Chaldean governor, Jer 40:6 comp. Neh 3:7, 19 . The same place is once written מִצְפֶּה ( Jos 18:26).
